name: Standard Deviation Calculator
description: Industrial-grade standard deviation calculator for process variability analysis, compliant with ISO 5725, Six Sigma, and Lean manufacturing standards.

inputs.label: Data Points (comma or space separated)
inputs.businessContext: Raw measurements from process output, e.g., dimensions, weights, cycle times.

inputs.label: Sample Size (n)
inputs.businessContext: Number of observations in the sample. Must be at least 2 for standard deviation calculation.

inputs.label: Data Type
inputs.businessContext: Continuous data (e.g., length, time) vs discrete (e.g., count of defects). Affects interpretation.

inputs.label: Population or Sample?
inputs.businessContext: Select 'population' if data covers entire population (n=N), else 'sample' for Bessel's correction.

inputs.label: Unit of Measure
inputs.businessContext: Physical unit of the data points (e.g., mm, sec, kg). Used for output labeling.

inputs.label: Lower Specification Limit (LSL)
inputs.businessContext: Lower bound of acceptable process output per engineering specifications.

inputs.label: Upper Specification Limit (USL)
inputs.businessContext: Upper bound of acceptable process output per engineering specifications.

inputs.label: Target Value (Nominal)
inputs.businessContext: Ideal process target, often the midpoint of specification limits.

rules.message: Sample size must be at least 2 to compute standard deviation.
Number of data points provided must equal the sample size.
Lower specification limit must be less than upper specification limit.
Target value should lie within specification limits for optimal process centering.

thresholds.message: Process variation exceeds 1/6 of tolerance band (Cp < 1.0). Improvement needed.
Process variation moderate (Cp between 1.0 and 1.33). Consider tightening.
Process mean is off-target by more than 0.5 sigma. Adjust centering.

formulas.name: Sum of Squared Deviations
formulas.description: Sum of squared differences from the mean.

formulas.name: Variance
formulas.formula: σ² = SS / n  (population)  or  s² = SS / (n-1) (sample)
formulas.description: Average squared deviation from the mean, with Bessel's correction for sample.

formulas.name: Standard Deviation
formulas.description: Square root of variance, in original units.

formulas.name: Relative Standard Deviation (RSD)
formulas.description: Coefficient of variation expressed as percentage.

formulas.name: Process Capability Index Cp
formulas.description: Measures potential process capability assuming centered process.

formulas.name: Process Capability Index Cpk
formulas.description: Measures actual process capability accounting for centering.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Process Variation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Standard deviation exceeds acceptable tolerance-based threshold, leading to increased defect rate and rework costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Off-Center Process Mean
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Mean deviates from target, causing asymmetric scrap and potential customer dissatisfaction.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Insufficient Sample Size
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Small sample size reduces confidence in standard deviation estimate, increasing risk of wrong decisions.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Process Variation
suggestedActions.description: Implement statistical process control (SPC) and root cause analysis to identify and eliminate sources of variation.

suggestedActions.label: Center Process on Target
suggestedActions.description: Adjust process parameters (e.g., tool offset, temperature) to shift mean toward target value.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Sample Size
suggestedActions.description: Collect more data points to improve reliability of standard deviation estimate and capability indices.

suggestedActions.label: Review Specification Limits
suggestedActions.description: If Cp/Cpk are low, consider tolerance relaxation or process redesign to meet requirements.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Standard Deviation
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Standard deviation adjusted for small sample bias using correction factor c4 (from ISO 5725).
